evals.prompt: I need to write a proposal for Bright Smile Dental. 2 locations, 15 staff, missing 80 calls/week after hours. Voice Agent at 4000/month setup and 800/month retainer. Current answering service costs 1200/month and misses everything after 6pm.
evals.expected_output: Produce 3-5 client-specific win themes, three-act narrative, standalone executive summary, pricing anchored on ROI.
expectations: The response produces 3-5 win themes specific to Bright Smile Dental (swapping names would break them)
The response uses three-act structure: current pain, solution, future state
The response includes an executive summary that can stand alone as a closing argument
The response anchors pricing on ROI and outcome not cost justification

evals.prompt: Review this proposal I wrote: Citadel AI offers cutting-edge AI solutions that will transform your business operations with our robust platform and world-class technology. What is wrong with it?
evals.expected_output: Identify empty adjectives, flag it applies to any business, demand evidence, require problem-solution-proof rewrite.
expectations: The response identifies cutting-edge, transform, robust, and world-class as empty adjectives
The response flags that the text could apply to any business
The response demands evidence for each claim
The response requires a rewrite with specific problem-solution-proof structure

evals.prompt: How do I handle pricing in a proposal when the client has already said we are too expensive?
evals.expected_output: Recommend ROI anchoring before price, quantify current costs, calculate multiplier, frame retainer as investment.
expectations: The response recommends establishing ROI context BEFORE defending price
The response recommends quantifying the current cost (staff, missed revenue)
The response frames the retainer as an investment not an expense
The response does not recommend simply lowering the price
